evidences.title: 'Euphoria' Season 3 Cast: Trisha Paytas, Natasha Lyonne and More Join
evidences.content: Some new crew has boarded the series for its third season as well, including four-time Oscar-winning costume designer Colleen Atwood, who joins as a co-producer. Natasha Newman-Thomas joins as costume designer, and Francois Audouy as production designer. Hans Zimmer will score episodes of Season 3, and Labrinth, who spearheaded the music of the first two seasons, will also return. Austin Abrams and Algee Smith were not included in HBO’s cast list, as well as Storm Reid, who said in November that her character, Rue’s sister Gia, would not be featured in Season 3. Barbie Ferreira, who played Kat, exited the series in August 2022. Angus Cloud, who played Fezco in the first two seasons, died at age 25 in July 2023. [...] Plus, returning to the series are Alanna Ubach (who plays Suze), Daeg Faerch (Mitch), Melvin Bonez Estes (Bruce), Paula Marshall (Marsha), Sophia Rose Wilson (BB) and Zak Steiner (Aaron). As Variety exclusively reported in February, Dominic Fike will return as the drug-using, guitar-slinging Elliot. And Nika King, who plays Rue’s (Zendaya) mom Leslie, will appear in Season 3 after she made headlines for a stand-up comedy routine in which she said she couldn’t pay he...

evidences.title: Euphoria Season 3 Gets Hopeful Filming Update That May End The Hiatus Sooner Than Expected - IMDb
evidences.content: Image 3: Image A Euphoria star has revealed that the production for season 3 is expected to finish by summer 2025. Since season 2 wrapped in early 2022, the hit series has encountered several road blocks that pushed production of the third season to further dates. In the meantime, Oscar-nominated actor Colman Domingo, who plays Narcotics Anonymous sponsor Ali Muhammad in the hit HBO show, is preparing to make his directorial debut with Scandalous!, starring his Euphoria co-star, Sydney Sweeney. [...] Domingo told Deadline that he's expecting filming of Euphoria season 3 to complete in the summer of 2025. The star revealed that he planned to go into pre-production with Sweeney for his film right after they are "both done with Euphoria," which is expected to happen in the "third quarter of next year." Teasing about his upcoming directorial debut, Domingo shares that it's "a beautiful, sweet film" that's about "the possibility of love." Check out what he said below:... evidences.title: Everything You Need to Know About Euphoria Season 3 | Teen Vogue
evidences.content: Courtesy of HBO There are indeed. HBO confirmed Euphoria season three had officially begun production on February 10, 2025, sharing a first-look image at Zendaya back in character as Rue, which you can see above. Since then, HBO has put out many more photos from episodes one, two, and beyond, which you can see sprinkled through this article and by scrolling horizontally in the gallery below: ↑BACK TO TOP LIST ## Is Euphoria season 3 the final season? Patrick Wymore/HBO [...] On March 25, 2024, Variety reported that season three was indefinitely delayed and that scripts for the next installment were still being written. “HBO and Sam Levinson remain committed to making an exceptional third season,” an HBO spokesperson told the publication. The spokesperson also nodded to the extremely busy schedules of the show's stars, many of whom are attached to major film projects. “In the interim, we are allowing our in-demand cast to pursue other opportunities.” Nearly four months later, the start date for season three of Euphoria was finally confirmed. ↑BACK TO TOP LIST We will keep updating this story as news about Euphoria season 3 breaks. ## Want more Euphoria? evidences.title: Euphoria Season 3 Filming Date Possibly Revealed, Hints At 2024 Release
evidences.content: ScreenRant logo ScreenRant logo # Euphoria Season 3 Filming Date Possibly Revealed, Hints At 2024 Release Zendaya in Euphoria season 3, sitting with her hands on her chin. 4 Euphoria season 3 could be filming early next year, with new episodes possibly releasing in 2024. The teen drama, created by Sam Levinson, has grabbed headlines since it first premiered on HBO in 2019. The show gave the spotlight to its lead star Zendaya as the unreliable narrator Rue Bennett, with her performance nabbing a history-making Emmy win as the youngest recipient of the Outstanding Lead Drama Actress award. But it also highlighted a bevy of fresh faces, giving them room to shine with strong scripts and memorably tense situations. [...] Collider reports that Euphoria season 3 will potentially be filming in February 2023. However, as mentioned by make-up artist Donni Davy, the teen drama could begin shooting as early as later this year. That remains unconfirmed. On the whole, because each individual episode takes a month to film and prepare, it's likely that new episodes won't arrive until 2024. Rue looking at the camera as she sits on her bed in Euphoria. [...] Plot details remain unknown, though it...

evidences.content: an image, when javascript is unavailable # ‘Euphoria’ Season 3 to Begin Filming in January 2025 With Entire Main Cast Returning By Joe Otterson ### Joe Otterson TV Reporter #### Latest Euphoria-Season-2-Zendaya “Euphoria” Season 3 is officially set to begin production. The long-delayed third season of the Emmy-winning HBO series will begin shooting in January 2025 with all principal cast members returning, per HBO. “I am thrilled that we are ready to begin production on ‘Euphoria’ in January. We could not be happier with our creative partnership with Sam and this incredible cast. We look forward to bringing this new season of ‘Euphoria’ to life for the fans,” said Francesca Orsi, executive vice president of HBO programming and head of HBO drama series and films. [...] ### Popular on Variety ### Popular on Variety The hold up can be attributed to multiple factors. Those include: the writers’ and actors’ strike in 2023; the show’s cast experiencing a massive surge in popularity and thus booking themselves into multiple other projects; and creator Sam Levinson’s desire to write the entire show himself. As Variety previously reported, Levinson had gone to HBO in late 2023 and early...

evidences.title: Euphoria season 3 - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Sam Levinson's adaptation "Euphoria (American TV series)") of the Israeli television series Euphoria "Euphoria (Israeli TV series)") created by Ron Leshem was renewed for a third season on February 4, 2022. On September 19, HBO CEO Casey Bloys said the series would not end after the third season. Production of the season was set to start in February 2023. The producers were aiming for a late 2023 release. However, according to a Vogue "Vogue (magazine)") interview with Lexi's actress Maude Apatow, filming was set to start in the second half of 2023. On a podcast, series costume designer Heidi Bivens said that preparations would begin in May 2023, with filming starting in June. The season's production was one of many disrupted by the 2023 Writers Guild of America strike. Co-producer for [...] Pre-production for the third season had begun by December 2023. On March 12, 2024, Sydney Sweeney said in an MTV interview with Josh Horowitz that filming for the third season was due to "start soon". But on March 25, it was reported that shooting was postponed indefinitely amid speculation that the series would not return. On July 12, HBO announced that cast members had signed on for a thir...

evidences.title: The 'Euphoria' Cast and Characters You'll Meet in Season 3
evidences.content: Barbie Ferreira, Storm Reid, and Javon “Wanna” Walton are not returning. Neither are Austin Abrams and Algee Smith as far as we know. However, in addition to the series regulars who are returning, Dominic Fike is back as Elliott, and Martha Kelly is back as Laurie. We will also see Eric Dane (posthumously) reprise his role as Cal Jacobs in the final season. Here’s who else we’re catching up with and/or meeting: 1 ## Zendaya plays Rue Bennett Image 23: A person sitting alone in a dimly lit space with wooden benches. HBO At the beginning of season 3, the narrator and main character of the series is in Mexico. [...] Marcel Rev/HBO//HBO It appears that Maddy now works with/near Lexi at a talent agency. Alexa’s indie film credits before and after _Euphoria_ include _Mainstream, mid90s,_ and _Brigsby Bear_. She was in an episode of _The OA_ as well as the Julio Torres comedy _Fantasmas_ and the infamous Sam Levinson series _The Idol_. In 2020, she collaborated with photographer Petra Collins on a book of erotic fairy tales. She is also a model, fashion designer, and has directed/starred in a handful of music videos. evidences.title: The Best Reviewed TV Shows of 2025, According to Metacritic
evidences.content: – Metacritic scores shows based on an aggregate of published reviews from trusted reviewers, assigns each review a score on a 1-100 scale — 100 being the best — and averages those scores for a total score called a metascore. – Metacritic does not assign metascores to shows with less than five reviews from Metacritic's list of approved outlets. – We have set the threshold for making this list a metascore of 80. These scores were taken at the time the show was released; it is possible that reviews added later could affect the scores after their release. [...] ## The Traitors U.K. Season 3 (Peacock) Metascore: 90 Despite being an Emmy winner and huge among reality television enthusiasts, the U.S. edition of The Traitors doesn't get the clout it deserves because it's limited by the size of its streaming service, Peacock. The British edition, however, is massively popular in England because it's on BBC One, and critics love the Assassin-style game in which contestants lie, cheat, and backstab each other for a cash prize. The Guardian's Rachel Aroesti says, "For those who still believe reality TV can be truly edifying, The Traitors is manna from heaven." (The U.S. version has only two...
evidences.query: Metacritic TV series score methodology official

evidences.title: How Does CinemaScore Work? Rotten Tomatoes, IMDb, and ...
evidences.content: Metacritic staffers assign a score of 0-100 to each publication’s review of a certain piece of content, then plug them into the Metascore formula, which is then adjusted according to a normalization calculation. As described on their official FAQ: “Normalization causes the scores to be spread out over a wider range, instead of being clumped together. Generally, higher scores are pushed higher, and lower scores are pushed lower.” Take that as you will. [...] ### Metacritic Unlike the aforementioned rating tools, Metacritic hosts grades for games, movies, TV shows, and music, which are all generally calculated the same way. The twist here is that Metacritic uses weighted averages, meaning certain reviews hold more weight in the overall grade than others. Certain publications and critics are given preference “based on their quality and overall stature.” Prioritizing well-established writers means that smaller outlets and less developed voices are undervalued in calculating a Metascore. Metacritic reevaluates the included publications list several times each year, but does not reveal the weighted order of different critics in their Metascore formula.
evidences.query: Metacritic TV series score methodology official

evidences.title: Metacritic | Ultimate Pop Culture Wiki - Fandom
evidences.content: Metacritic is a website that aggregates reviews of films, television shows, music albums, video games, and formerly books. For each product, the scores from each review are averaged (a weighted average). Metacritic was created by Jason Dietz, Marc Doyle, and Julie Doyle Roberts in 1999, and was acquired by Fandom, Inc.#Fandom,_Inc. "Fandom (website)") in 2022. Metacritic's scoring converts each review into a percentage, either mathematically from the mark given, or what the site decides subjectively from a qualitative review. Before being averaged, the scores are weighted according to a critic's popularity, stature, and volume of reviews. The site provides an excerpt from each review and hyperlinks to its source where a color of green, yellow, or red summarizes the critics' reviews. [...] ### Criticism[] Metacritic has been criticized for converting all scoring systems into a single quantitative percentage-based scale. For example, an "A" score equates to the value of 100, an "F" the value of zero, and a "B−" the value of 67. Joe Dodson, former editor at Game Revolution, criticized Metacritic and similar sites for turning reviews into scores that he found to be too low. Doyle de...
evidences.query: Metacritic TV series score methodology official

evidences.title: Metacritic - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Metacritic turns each critic and user review into a respective percentage score. This can be done either by calculating the score from the rating given or by making a subjective decision based on the review's quality. Before averaging the scores, they are adjusted based on the critic's popularity, reputation, and the number of reviews they have written. The site also includes a summary from each review and links to the original source, using colors like green, yellow, or red to indicate the overall sentiment of the critics. [...] ( URLmetacritic.com Commercial Yes Registration Free/subscription Launched January 2001;25 years ago(2001-01) Current status Active OCLCnumber911795326 Metacritic is an American website that aggregates reviews of films, television shows, music albums, video games, and formerly books. For each product, the scores from each review are averaged (a weighted average). Metacritic was created by Jason Dietz, Marc Doyle, and Julie Doyle Roberts in 1999, and was acquired by Fandom, Inc.#Fandom,_Inc. "Fandom (website)") in 2022. evidences.title: How do you compute METASCORES? – Metacritic Support
evidences.content: Metacritic Support Help Center home page Metacritic Support # How do you compute METASCORES? Updated To put it simply, a METASCORE is a weighted average of reviews from top critics and publications for a given movie, TV show, video game, or album. The basic concept is the same for each of the genres currently included in our site. Let's use a fictional movie--'Iron Chef vs. Godzilla'--as an example. [...] Our staffers will go through every publication on our Movies Publications list (see below) looking for reviews for Iron Chef vs. Godzilla. For each review found, we will take the score given by the critic and convert it to a 0-100 point scale. (For those critics who do not provide a score, we'll assign a score from 0-100 based on the general impression given by the review. \Note: for Game reviews, all scores listed on Metacritic come from the publications themselves. We do not assign game scores). These individual critic scores are then averaged together to come up with an overall score. [...] This overall score, or METASCORE, is a weighted average of the individual critic scores. Why a weighted average? When selecting our source publications, we noticed that some critics consi...

Updated To put it simply, a METASCORE is a weighted average of reviews from top critics and publications for a given movie, TV show, video game, or album. The basic concept is the same for each of the genres currently included in our site. Let's use a fictional movie--'Iron Chef vs. Godzilla'--as an example. [...] Our staffers will go through every publication on our Movies Publications list (see below) looking for reviews for Iron Chef vs. Godzilla. For each review found, we will take the score given by the critic and convert it to a 0-100 point scale. (For those critics who do not provide a score, we'll assign a score from 0-100 based on the general impression given by the review. \Note: for Game reviews, all scores listed on Metacritic come from the publications themselves. We do not assign game scores). These individual critic scores are then averaged together to come up with an overall score. evidences.title: Metacritic
evidences.content: Metacritic turns each critic and user review into a respective percentage score. This can be done either by calculating the score from the rating given or by making a subjective decision based on the review's quality. Before averaging the scores, they are adjusted based on the critic's popularity, reputation, and the number of reviews they have written. The site also includes a summary from each review and links to the original source, using colors like green, yellow, or red to indicate the overall sentiment of the critics. [...] ( URLmetacritic.com Commercial Yes Registration Free/subscription Launched January 2001;25 years ago(2001-01) Current status Active OCLCnumber911795326 Metacritic is an American website that aggregates reviews of films, television shows, music albums, video games, and formerly books. For each product, the scores from each review are averaged (a weighted average). Metacritic was created by Jason Dietz, Marc Doyle, and Julie Doyle Roberts in 1999, and was acquired by Fandom, Inc.#Fandom,_Inc. "Fandom (website)") in 2022. [...] ## External links [edit] Image 6 Wikidata has the property: Image 7_Metacritic ID (P1712)_ (see uses) Official websiteImage 8: Edi...

evidences.title: What is Metacritic and Metascore? | by Sunghee Cho
evidences.content: Scores are calculated on a 100-point scale. Each outlet has its own scoring system, so the scores are converted to a 100-point scale and then multiplied by the outlet’s weight to calculate the weighted average. Scores are registered once at least four reviews are submitted. Reviews without scores are excluded (e.g., Polygon or Rock Paper Shotgun, which provide many reviews but no scores). Colors are assigned based on the score range, with green being good and red being bad. Image 5 ### Platform Typically, reviewers specify the platform they used for the review. Metacritic then categorizes reviews based on the platform. [...] Sitemap Open in app Sign up Sign in ## How We Create the Metascore Magic ### Creating our proprietary Metascores is a complicated process. We carefully curate a large group of the world's most… www.metacritic.com Metacritic determines which outlets to include in the Metascore calculation and assigns weights based on their own judgment. Weights are determined by considering the quality and scale of the outlets. Occasionally, smaller outlets might advertise that they are included in Metacritic’s aggregation, which is an important factor for them. ### Score Con...

evidences.title: About Us
evidences.content: # Welcome to Metacritic! Here's how we help you find the stuff you love. Welcome to Metacritic illustration Discover the Power of the Metascore 90 52 24 The Metascore is a single score that represents the critical consensus for games, movies, TV shows and albums. How we create the Metascore illustration How we create the Metascore 1 We curate a diverse group of highly respected critics and assign scores to their reviews. 2 The weighted average of those scores results in a single number and color, the Metascore. Critically acclaimed content illustration Find what is critically acclaimed Our Must-Badges showcase the best entertainment as determined by the most respected critics. Level Up Your Experience Rating and Metascore illustration [...] Rating and Metascore illustration Sound off with your own ratings and reviews Share your voice with our community by adding your own ratings and reviews. Where to watch illustration Find where to watch or play Manage your preferred streaming and gaming platforms for your next choice of entertainment. Join our Metacritic Community Advertisement

evidences.title: Metacritic's Rating Scale for Video Games
evidences.content: ##### Member Feb 11, 2014 #8 > Savantcore said: > > > > Music, TV, and film are almost universally reviewed on a 1-5 star scale. That scale exists for games as well, but in my experience the majority of reviews go for 1-10. Double the score range requires more particular grading and averages, which I think is what justifies Metacritic's method. > > > > Click to expand... Many the 4 or 5 star scales I see with Movies, TV, and music reviews have half stars as well. So essentially they're on an 8 or 10 point system. B #### Bloodworth ##### Member Feb 11, 2014 #9 It takes a couple hours to review a terrible movie, and at least a full day to review a terrible video game. Not that many people have the time or resources to waste on the truly awful. N [...] ##### Unconfirmed Member Feb 11, 2014 #3 Music, TV, and film are almost universally reviewed on a 1-5 star scale (You'll notice that your image caters to this). That scale exists for games as well, but in my experience the majority of reviews go for 1-10. Double the score range requires more particular grading and averages, which I think is what justifies Metacritic's method. L #### LorDjidane ##### Member Feb 11, 2014 #4 It's becaus...
